name: Teuler Mines Trail
name_original: Carril cicloturista Minas de Teuler – circular desde Santa Olalla de Cala
description: This 24.1-mile difficult touring cycling route from Santa Olalla de Cala explores historic Minas de Teuler and Sierra de Aracena's dehesas.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Santa Olalla Del Cala traverse the southern slopes of the Sierra Morena mountains, situated within the Sierra de Aracena y Picos de Aroche Natural Park. The region features diverse landscapes, including pastures, small hills, and extensive woodlands of holm oaks and cork oaks. Cyclists can expect varied terrain, from moderate paths on the Vía de la Plata to more challenging routes with significant elevation gains, often mixing paved and unpaved surfaces. Best touring cycling routes around Santa Olalla Del Cala The most popular touring cycling route is Vía de la Plata - Castles of Santa Olalla and El Real de la Jara Loop, a 22.7 miles (36.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 15 minutes to complete. This route explores the historic Vía de la Plata and passes by the castles of Santa Olalla and El Real de la Jara. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Teuler Mines Trail, a difficult 24.1 miles (38.8 km) path. This trail leads through historic mining landscapes, offering insights into the region's industrial past. Local touring cyclists also love the El Real de la Jara and Almadén de la Plata Loop, a 23.3 miles (37.6 km) trail leading through the natural park and connecting nearby towns, often completed in about 2 hours 56 minutes. Touring cycling around Santa Olalla Del Cala is defined by the Sierra de Aracena Natural Park, the Dehesa Landscape, and historic mining areas. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate loops to difficult routes with substantial climbs. The routes in Santa Olalla Del Cala are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Santa Olalla Del Cala's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Santa Olalla Del Cala?
A: There are over 30 touring cycling routes around Santa Olalla Del Cala. These routes cater to various skill levels, with options ranging from moderate loops to more challenging rides with significant elevation gains.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in this region?
A: The terrain around Santa Olalla Del Cala is quite varied. You'll encounter a mix of paved and unpaved surfaces, traversing the diverse landscapes of the Sierra de Aracena y Picos de Aroche Natural Park. Expect pastures, small hills, and extensive woodlands of holm oaks and cork oaks, with some routes exploring historic mining areas or following ancient paths like the Vía de la Plata.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes for beginners or those seeking a relaxed ride?
A: While many routes in the area are considered difficult, there are options for less experienced cyclists. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021943414/via-de-la-plata-castles-of-santa-olalla-and-el-real-de-la-jara-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía de la Plata - Castles of Santa Olalla and El Real de la Jara Loop&lt;/a&gt; is rated as moderate, offering a good balance of scenic beauty and manageable challenge.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural features to see along the cycling routes?
A: Cyclists can explore a rich blend of natural and historical attractions. You might pass by the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5585975" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cala Castle&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy panoramic views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/650365" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro del Calvario Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5392543" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rivera de Cala&lt;/a&gt; and the unique Dehesa Landscape, an agro-forestry system with ancient holm oaks.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the region's mining history?
A: Yes, the region has a rich mining past. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984554418/teuler-mines-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Teuler Mines Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging route that specifically leads through historic mining landscapes, offering a glimpse into the area's industrial heritage.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Santa Olalla Del Cala?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 10 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, the blend of natural beauty with cultural exploration, and the opportunity to traverse the unique Dehesa Landscape.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Santa Olalla Del Cala are designed as loops. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988128215/el-real-de-la-jara-and-almaden-de-la-plata-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Real de la Jara and Almadén de la Plata Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which connects nearby towns, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991641024/almaden-de-la-plata-and-melonares-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almadén de la Plata and Melonares Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by castles or historic sites?
A: Absolutely. The region is dotted with historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021943414/via-de-la-plata-castles-of-santa-olalla-and-el-real-de-la-jara-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía de la Plata - Castles of Santa Olalla and El Real de la Jara Loop&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, passes by the historic castles of Santa Olalla and El Real de la Jara. You can also find routes near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3754437" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Towers&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Santa Olalla Del Cala?
A: Given its location in Andalusia, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant weather for touring cycling, with milder temperatures and lush landscapes. Summers can be very hot, while winters are typically mild but can have more rainfall.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of reservoirs or significant water features?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate views of the region's water bodies. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991641024/almaden-de-la-plata-and-melonares-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almadén de la Plata and Melonares Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging route that includes the Melonares Reservoir, offering scenic waterside cycling.

Q: What is the average duration for a touring cycling route in this area?
A: The duration of routes varies significantly based on distance and difficulty. For example, a moderate route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021943414/via-de-la-plata-castles-of-santa-olalla-and-el-real-de-la-jara-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía de la Plata - Castles of Santa Olalla and El Real de la Jara Loop&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 2 hours 15 minutes, while more difficult and longer routes can take 4 hours or more, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20122086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesa Landscape – Castle of the Towers loop from Santa Olalla del Cala&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any specific natural monuments or unique trees to look out for?
A: Yes, the region is home to the Monumento Natural Encina de la Dehesa de San Francisco, which features an ancient and expansive holm oak estimated to be between 400 and 500 years old. While not directly on a listed route, it's a significant natural landmark in the area's characteristic Dehesa landscape.
tips: It is a beautiful path through the Sierra de Aracena and Picos de Aroche natural park.

It is a beautiful lagoon that was once one of the facilities of the Teuler mines.

These mines were worked by Germans and very close are the ruins of the houses that were built to house the engineers who worked here.

Santa Olalla del Cala Castle is an impressive fortress located in the town of Santa Olalla del Cala, in the province of Huelva, Andalusia. This castle, of Muslim origin, was built in the 13th century and later renovated by the Christians after the Reconquista.
The castle is located in a strategic position, on top of a hill, which allowed it to control the passage between Seville and the Sierra de Aracena. Its structure includes robust walls, defensive towers and a parade ground. The keep is one of the most outstanding elements of the castle, offering panoramic views of the surroundings.
Today, Santa Olalla del Cala Castle is an important tourist attraction and a symbol of the historical heritage of the region. Guided tours are available that allow visitors to explore its corners and learn more about its history and architecture.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Cycling route Minas de Teuler – circular from Santa Olalla de Cala?
faqs.answer: This route is officially rated as difficult for touring cyclists. While some sections, particularly those following the old mining railway, have gentle slopes and good surfaces, the overall route includes a total ascent of approximately 555 meters over its 38.8 km distance. It's manageable for those with a good fitness level, and electric bicycles can make the inclines much easier.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter a varied terrain, reflecting its past as an old mining railway line. Expect a mix of gravel tracks, dirt paths, and some paved or asphalted sections. The path can be quite sinuous in places, framed by dry-stone walls, and you'll experience both practically horizontal stretches and gentle climbs.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers several notable sights. You'll cycle through well-preserved cork oak and holm oak forests, and pass by the impressive "Alcornoque de las Minas de Teuler," a Singular Tree of Andalusia. Key highlights include the Fantastic dirt road, the Teuler Mines Viewpoint overlooking the open-pit mine (now a small lake), and the historic Santa Olalla Castle.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route shares sections with several other trails. You'll find yourself on parts of the Transandalus, the Sendero de Sierra Morena, and the Camino de Santa Olalla a Corteconcepción, among others.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best times to cycle are typically spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot in Andalusia, making cycling less comfortable, especially on longer routes. Winter can be pleasant, but check local weather forecasts for rain.

faqs.question: Is the Cycling route Minas de Teuler – circular from Santa Olalla de Cala dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, trails in the Sierra de Aracena Natural Park are dog-friendly, but it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through livestock areas or near wildlife. Be mindful of the varied terrain and potential for hot surfaces in summer.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a specific permit to cycle this route, nor is there an entrance fee. The trail is publicly accessible and runs through the Parque Natural Sierra de Aracena y Picos de Aroche, which is open to visitors without charge. However, always respect local regulations regarding nature conservation.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead in Santa Olalla de Cala?
faqs.answer: The route starts directly from Santa Olalla de Cala, making it easily accessible. You should be able to find parking within the town, particularly near the main access points or public parking areas. Look for designated parking spots or consult local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or pubs near the route?
faqs.answer: Santa Olalla de Cala, being the starting and ending point, offers amenities such as cafes and pubs where you can refuel before or after your ride. Along the route itself, especially once you leave the town, facilities will be scarce, so plan accordingly.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife or plants might I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route traverses the rich dehesa ecosystem of the Sierra de Aracena Natural Park. You'll be surrounded by cork oak and holm oak forests, fig trees, wild olive trees, and various Mediterranean shrubs like white heather, wild roses, and lavender. Keep an eye out for raptors and other local wildlife that inhabit this protected area.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 38.8 km long with over 550 meters of elevation gain. For a touring cyclist, it typically takes around 3 hours and 16 minutes of active cycling time. However, allow extra time for stops at viewpoints, exploring the mine area, and enjoying the scenery.
